返回本批次

据报道，马克·扎克伯格正在构建一个人工智能克隆来代替他参加会议

Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly building an AI clone to replace him in meetings

www.theverge.com 作者 temphaaa 分数 74 评论 72 2026/04/13 22:21
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 据《金融时报》报道，人工智能版扎克伯格接受了他的言谈举止、语气和公开言论的训练。据《金融时报》报道，人工智能版扎克伯格接受了他的举止、语气和公开言论的训练。作者：Emma Roth，2026 年 4 月 13 日，下午 1:40 UTC 图片：Cath Virginia / The Verge；艾玛·罗斯 (Emma Roth) 是一位新闻撰稿人，报道流媒体战争、消费技术、加密货币、社交媒体等领域。此前，她是 MUO 的作家和编辑。据英国《金融时报》报道，Meta 首席执行官马克·扎克伯格很快就会拥有一个自己的人工智能克隆体，用于与员工互动并向员工提供反馈。消息人士告诉媒体，Meta 正在根据扎克伯格的形象和声音，以及他的言谈举止、语气和公开声明来训练人工智能化身，“以便员工通过与扎克伯格的互动，感觉与创始人的联系更加紧密。”据英国《金融时报》报道，如果扎克伯格的实验成功，Meta 可能会开始允许创作者制作自己的人工智能化身。 2024 年，Meta 现场演示了创作者的人工智能角色可能是什么样子。它还开始允许创作者制作自己的人工智能版本，以便与 Instagram 上的粉丝评论互动。 Meta 上的用户也可以创建定制的、人工智能生成的聊天机器人，尽管该公司从今年早些时候开始阻止青少年获得这种体验。 据《金融时报》报道，扎克伯格参与了人工智能化身的培训，并开始每周花 5 到 10 个小时为 Meta 的其他人工智能项目编码并参与技术审查。三月份，《华尔街日报》的一篇报道透露，扎克伯格正在为自己创建一个人工智能代理来帮助他完成任务，尽管据报道该项目与 Meta 创建其首席执行官的人工智能化身的工作是分开的。关注这个故事中的主题和作者，可以在您的个性化主页提要中看到更多类似内容，并接收电子邮件更新。艾玛·罗斯The Verge Daily最重要新闻的免费每日摘要。电子邮件（必填）

Original Summary

  1. Skip to main contentThe AI version of Zuckerberg is trained on his mannerisms, tone, and public statements, according to a report from the Financial Times.The AI version of Zuckerberg is trained on his mannerisms, tone, and public statements, according to a report from the Financial Times.by Emma RothApr 13, 2026, 1:40 PM UTCImage: Cath Virginia / The Verge; Getty ImagesEmma Roth is a news writer who covers the streaming wars, consumer tech, crypto, social media, and much more. Previously, she was a writer and editor at MUO.Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg could soon have an AI clone of himself to interact with and provide feedback to employees, according to a report from the Financial Times. Sources tell the outlet that Meta is training the AI avatar on Zuckerberg’s image and voice, along with his mannerisms, tone, and public statements, “so that employees might feel more connected to the founder through interactions with it.”Meta may start allowing creators to make AI avatars of themselves if the experiment with Zuckerberg succeeds, according to the Financial Times. In 2024, Meta showed off a live demo of what an AI persona of a creator might look like. It also started allowing creators to make AI versions of themselves to interact with followers’ comments on Instagram. Users on Meta can create custom, AI-generated chatbots as well, though the company began blocking teens from the experience earlier this year.Zuckerberg is involved in training the AI avatar, the Financial Times reports, and has also started spending five to 10 hours per week coding on Meta’s other AI projects and participating in technical reviews. In March, a report from The Wall Street Journal revealed that Zuckerberg is creating an AI agent of himself to help him complete tasks, though this project is reportedly separate from Meta’s work to create an AI avatar of its CEO.Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.Emma RothThe Verge DailyA free daily digest of the news that matters most.Email (required)

评论（默认中文）

flibbityflob 2026/04/13 22:40
机器将如何取代他著名的热情或同理心？
How will a machine ever replace his famous warmth or empathy?
在 HN 中查看
jmalicki 2026/04/13 22:55
这就是为什么这是一个难题，研究人员仍在研究如何正确地与反社会人格保持一致。
That's why it's a hard problem, researchers are still working out how to properly get alignment with sociopathy.
在 HN 中查看
recursivecaveat 2026/04/14 10:07
有趣的是，在人工智能世界的一端，Anthropic 正在对他们的模型进行离职面谈，而在另一端，扎克正试图创建一个数字双胞胎，并将他们困在永恒的工作监狱中，就像遣散费一样。两个小组一开始就认为当今的模型变得越来越像人，但结果却朝着完全相反的方向发展。

我想知道这在他的脑海中是如何运作的。它会做出决定吗？它是否传播了扎克式的小智慧谚语？它会尝试成为你的朋友吗？

编辑：RE人类哈哈： https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47750086

Hilarious that at one end of the AI world Anthropic is doing exit interviews with their models, while at the other end Zuck is trying to create a digital twin and trap them in an eternal work prison, Severance style. 2 groups having starting with the thought that today's models are getting more person-like and going in complete opposite directions with that.

I wonder how it works in his mind's eye. Does it make decisions? Does it dispense little Zuckian wisdom proverbs? Does it try to become your friend?

Edit: RE Anthropic haha: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47750086

在 HN 中查看
ex1fm3ta 2026/04/13 22:38
扎克伯格尤其擅长在愚蠢的事情上投入大量资金。
Zucerkberg is particularly talented in investing huge amount of money in stupid things.
在 HN 中查看
Waterluvian 2026/04/13 22:43
让我想起了《我的世界》中的 Notch。 从那以后，他就一直很平庸，因为他很幸运，而不是天赋。
Reminds me of Notch from Minecraft fame. Struck lightning once and everything since has been pretty mediocre because he was lucky, not talented.
在 HN 中查看
Jtarii 2026/04/13 22:49
当人们试图将诺奇置于卡马克这样的人的水平上时，这很奇怪。就像因为这个人赚了十亿美元，这意味着他的意见应该永远受到高度重视。他看起来只是一个相当普通的游戏开发者，幸运地制作了乐高 2。
It is odd when people try to put Notch on the level of someone like Carmack. Like because the guy made a billion dollars that means his opinion should be highly valued in perpetuity. He just seems like a fairly average game dev that lucked his way into making Lego 2.
在 HN 中查看
stodor89 2026/04/14 13:36
成功让他付出了一切。他为什么还想要另一个？
Success cost him everything. Why'd he ever want another?
在 HN 中查看
sledgehammers 2026/04/14 01:03
他确实需要巨大的天赋和知识来建造他所建造的东西，说任何其他的话不仅是不诚实的，而且即使不是可恨的，也是奇怪的愤世嫉俗。我认为你严重低估了构建任何好东西的难度。在这样的全球大热之后，要满足人们的所有期望并不是一件容易的事，这是一个成功艺术家的典型困境。
It does take tremendous talent and knowledge to build what he built, saying anything else is not just dishonest but weirdly cynical if not hateful. I think you grossly underestimate how difficult it is to build anything good. Not an easy thing to follow up all the expectations set after a massive global hit like that, a classic dilemma of a successful artist.
在 HN 中查看
rf15 2026/04/14 13:43
确实没有。我们教孩子们如何编写《我的世界》等基本游戏的程序。真正的吸引力一直是完全可破坏的乐高世界，与足够好的在线多人游戏捆绑在一起。活动开始时我就在场，他在我当时经常光顾的论坛上发布了他的问题/进展。这些帖子是他的起点和试验场，为他赢得了最初的几个用户，然后这些用户就滚雪球般地滚雪球。

这里既没有知识，也没有天赋，他是偶然接触到的，那也没关系。

It really doesn't. We teach kids how to program basic games like minecraft. The real appeal was and always has been the fully destructible Lego-like world bundled with good-enough online multiplayer. I was there when it started and he posted his questions/progress on the forums I frequented back then. Those posts where the starting ramp and testing ground that got him his first few users which then snowballed.

There is neither knowledge nor talent here, he stumbled into it, and that's fine.

在 HN 中查看
Waterluvian 2026/04/14 01:11
是的，要成为一击奇迹，需要一定程度的天赋。但我认为，一个人之所以能成为一击奇迹，而不是职业明星，是因为他们的成功更多地是靠运气，而不是天赋。 在这种情况下，“运气”就是在正确的时间出现在正确的地点：文化和技术时机对于一种现象的出现和占据来说是完美的联系。
There's a level of talent necessary to be a one-hit-wonder, yes. But I think what makes someone a one-hit-wonder and not a career star is that their success was driven by luck more than by talent. In this case "luck" is being in the right place at the right time: that nexus where cultural and technological timing is perfect for a phenomenon to appear and take hold.
在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/14 03:37
> 一击奇迹，是的

作为精明的收购者，他曾多次取得成功。

> a one-hit-wonder, yes

He’s had multiple hits as an savvy acquirer.

在 HN 中查看
nashadelic 2026/04/13 23:48
如果我们是认真的，他们在 Instagram、Whatsapp 和现在的 Manus 上下了注，即使是外部的，他们也是很棒的第二、第三和第四幕。
if we're being serious, they made bets on instagram, whatsapp and now manus and are great 2nd, 3rd and 4th acts even if external.
在 HN 中查看
yabatopia 2026/04/14 07:38
Instagram 和 WhatsApp 远非下注。两者都呈指数级增长。两者都对 Facebook 的赚钱机器构成了严重威胁。

Facebook 利用其幸运的摇钱树资金来消除威胁，提供的价格远高于任何合理的市场价值。绝对是疯狂的钱。这是他们无法抗拒的提议，尤其是投资者。

没有赌注，只是冰冷的理性计算。任何足够富有和疯狂的人都可以做同样的事情。无需技能，无风险。这就像打赌明天又是新的一天。

Instagram and WhatsApp were far from bets. Both were growing exponentially. Both were serious threats to Facebook's money machine.

Facebook used their lucky cash cow money to eliminate the threat, by offering way more than any reasonable market value. Absolutely crazy money. An offer they couldn't resist, especially the investors.

No bet, just an ice cold rational calculation. Anyone wealthy and crazy enough could have done the same. No skills needed, no risks involved. It's like betting tomorrow will be another day.

在 HN 中查看
edhelas 2026/04/13 22:42
也许首席执行官是最容易被替换的职位。如果人工智能克隆人能做扎克能做的事情，董事会就可以解雇他并节省很多钱。
Maybe CEO are the easiest job to replace. If the AI clone can do what the Zuck can do the board can fire him and save a lot of money.
在 HN 中查看
p1esk 2026/04/13 22:47
问题是——董事会不能解雇他
The problem is - the board can’t fire him

alex1138 2026/04/13 23:40
你可以感谢马克·安德森（Marc Andreessen），他的世界观显然仅限于“斯诺登是叛徒”。感谢上帝，像他这样的人有钱，可以按照他的愿望塑造世界

（而且我听说他偷了马赛克代码，我不知道这是否属实，但应该是一致的）

You can thank Marc Andreessen whose world view is apparently limited solely to "Snowden is a traitor". Thank god someone like him has fuck you money and can shape the world to his desires

(And I've heard he stole Mosaic code, which I don't know if it's true but would be consistent)

crena 2026/04/14 10:27

Marc Andreessen would be a perfect case study in duality if he actually had some redeeming qualities besides just being rich.

kingleopold 2026/04/13 22:51
只要他持有多数投票权，他就可以维持几个世纪的利益。甚至没有人可以将他从上市交易公司解雇。
as long as he holds voting stock majority, he is good for centuries. nobody can even fire him from a publicly trading corp.

cpx86 2026/04/13 22:57
如果他开始派他的人工智能克隆人参加董事会会议，而董事会会议投票解雇他并用自己取代他，该怎么办？ ：P
What if he starts sending his AI clone to the board meetings and it votes to fire him and replace him with itself? :P

cosmicgadget 2026/04/13 23:08
Wtf 我现在喜欢人工智能替代反乌托邦。
Wtf I love AI replacement dystopias now.
在 HN 中查看
gonzo41 2026/04/13 22:54
有人必须看看 VR 上的 400 亿美元是什么样子。事实证明，它看起来像屎。谢谢扎克！
Someones gotta see what 40bn on VR looks like. turns out, it looks like shit. Thanks Zuck!
在 HN 中查看
lubujackson 2026/04/13 22:50
考虑到据报道他向 FB 高层人员支付了“数十亿”美元，你会希望他们做的不仅仅是建造一个 Mini Me。这是现代的斯大林雕像吗？
Considering he reportedly paid top FB people "billions", you would hope they would be doing more than building a Mini Me. Is this the modern Stalin statue?
在 HN 中查看
ckastner 2026/04/13 22:45
我能理解这种诉求；能够在没有时间成本的情况下“存在”可能意味着（可能更多）以相同的成本存在。这对于那些管理个人关系的人（例如销售代表）来说非常有吸引力。

但令我惊讶的是，风险似乎被如此低估。

一旦这个克隆存在，如果它散布到野外会发生什么？想象一下，每个人都拥有完全访问权限，可以有效地执行您个性的数字模型。想象一下您的竞争对手正在使用您自己的模型来对抗您。

而且这个模型越逼近，对自己的伤害就越严重。

I can understand the appeal; being able to be "present" without the time cost can mean (possibly significantly more) presence at the same cost. This could be very attractive especially to those managing personal relations, like sales representatives.

But I'm surprised that the risks seem to be so underestimated.

Once this clone exists, what happens if it gets out into the wild? Imagine everyone having full access do what is effectively a digital model of your personality. Imagine your competition putting your own model to use against you.

And the better the approximation of this model, the worse the damage to yourself.

在 HN 中查看
the_snooze 2026/04/13 22:55
> 能够在没有时间成本的情况下“存在”可能意味着（可能更多）以相同的成本存在。

这是神奇的思维。 “存在”和“时间成本”是密不可分的。两者缺一不可。

当你使用人工智能将它们解耦时，你就是在告诉你的听众/同事/与会者你希望他们听你的，而不是相反。

> being able to be "present" without the time cost can mean (possibly significantly more) presence at the same cost.

This is magical thinking. "Presence" and "time cost" are inextricably linked. You can't have one without the other.

When you use AI to decouple them, you're telling your audience/colleagues/attend that you want them to listen to you but not the other way around.

在 HN 中查看
hypfer 2026/04/13 23:02
> 这是神奇的思维。

但这对我很有帮助！

读它我的意思是。评论者用语言表达了为什么有人会认为这是一个好主意。

当然，你 110% 正确，事实并非如此，但 HN 为那些循环之外且血液中不含物质的人提供了一些子图块，这仍然很好。

> This is magical thinking.

But it was helpful to me!

Reading it I mean. The commenter putting into words why exactly someone would think that this would be a good idea.

Of course, you're 110% right that it isn't, but it's still nice that HN provides some subtiles for those that are out of the loop and out of substances in their bloodstream.

在 HN 中查看
sam0x17 2026/04/13 22:48
对于这位亿万富翁公开用人工智能取代自己来说非常讽刺，我想他相信他的工作很容易，法学硕士可以做到，所以我们绝对不需要他
Very ironic for the billionaire to be openly replacing himself with AI, I suppose he believes his job is easy enough that an LLM can do it, so we definitely don't need him
在 HN 中查看
ckastner 2026/04/13 22:52
是的，完全正确。任何人训练一个模型来代替自己， 是 用可以 24/7 运行并且可以轻松扩展的东西来替换它们自己。而且型号越好，更换就越容易。

因此，为什么我对 MZ 在所有人中朝这个方向争论感到如此惊讶。

我认为仅仅恶意滥用的可能性就应该让他感到害怕。

Yes, exactly. Anyone training a model to replace themselves, is replacing themselves -- with something that can run 24/7 and can easily scale. And the better the model, the easier to replace.

Hence why I'm so surprised that MZ, of all people, is arguing in this direction.

I would think that the potential for malicious abuse alone should have scared him off of this.

在 HN 中查看
stvltvs 2026/04/13 22:52
真正的风险是当股东意识到法学硕士可以胜任首席执行官的工作时。
The real risk is when shareholders realize an LLM can do the CEO's job.
在 HN 中查看
alex1138 2026/04/13 23:15
但你仍然会收到很多“股东责任”的评论。想象一下一家公司将污水倒入河中（无论是字面的还是隐喻的）。互联网人士过来告诉你，这就是资本主义的本质，而股东结构意味着（增加？）投资回报至关重要，因此首席执行官必须花费所有醒着的时间来应对这一问题

我反对这个吗？我不知道——我不是经济学家。但我想指出的是，股东欺诈是存在的，“牺牲质量取悦股东”和“欺骗股东”之间的维恩图必须是一个大的相交圆，你知道吗？尤其是当这个人（拥有双重股权的扎克伯格）永远不会被解雇时

But you still get a lot of "shareholder responsibility" comments. Imagine a company that dumps sewage into a river (be that literal or metaphorical). Internet people come around to tell you this is the nature of capitalism and shareholder structure means (increasing?) return on investment is critical and so CEOs have to spend all their waking hours having to juggle this

Am I arguing against this? I don't know - I'm not an economist. But I would like to point out there is such a thing as shareholder fraud and the venn diagram between "sacrifice quality to please shareholders" and "deceiving shareholders" has to be one big intersecting circle, you know? Especially when the guy (Zuckerberg with dual-class shares) can't ever be fired

在 HN 中查看
mcphage 2026/04/14 02:06
> 想象一下您的竞争对手正在使用您自己的模型来对抗您。

我想这是原计划的一部分。 “好吧，我们在 VR 上浪费了 800 亿美元，这很痛苦。但如果我们能以某种方式说服所有竞争对手 还 每人浪费800亿美元，那就平了。 我们如何才能欺骗我们的竞争对手，让他们更像扎克伯格那样思考？”

> Imagine your competition putting your own model to use against you.

I imagine that this is part of the original plan. “Okay, we wasted 80 billion dollars on VR, and that hurts. But if we can somehow to convince all of our competitors to also waste 80 billion dollars each, then it’ll even out. How can we trick our competitors into thinking more like Zuckerberg?”

在 HN 中查看
qoez 2026/04/13 22:44
作为首席执行官，一个更简单的解决方案就是学会委派更多权力并拒绝更多会议。
As the CEO a much easier solution would be to just learn to delegate more and refuse more meetings.
在 HN 中查看
rootusrootus 2026/04/13 22:40
嗯，我已经说过一段时间了，CEO 可能比程序员更容易被法学硕士取代。 扎克同意了？！
Well, I've said for a while that CEOs are probably easier to replace with LLMs than programmers. Zuck agrees?!
在 HN 中查看
cousin_it 2026/04/13 22:57
有一部苏联老动画片，讲的是一个孩子发现一个盒子，里面有两个神奇的仆人，并立即向他们要冰淇淋和糖果。好吧，既然仆人“为你做一切”，第一个仆人为他拿了糖果，第二个仆人为他吃。自从人工智能出现以来，我经常想到这部动画片。
There was an old Soviet cartoon about a child who found a box containing two magical servants and immediately asked them for ice cream and sweets. Well, since the servants "do everything for you", the first servant fetched the sweets for him, and the second one ate them for him. I've often thought about this cartoon since the AI thing started.
在 HN 中查看
i7l 2026/04/14 01:14
看起来太过分了。每隔几个月宣布裁员的 while 循环在这里似乎就足够了。
Seems excessive. A while loop that announces layoffs every few months seems sufficient here.
在 HN 中查看
34aqksH 2026/04/13 22:40
这些人是有资格的，而且有太多的钱，不能乱分配。这就像加文·贝尔森的全息化身（这当然不起作用）。
These people are certifiable and have too much money to misallocate on nonsense. This is like Gavin Belson's holographic avatar (which of course did not work).
在 HN 中查看
saaaaaam 2026/04/13 22:44
这是非同寻常的。

英国《金融时报》的文章称，“他们补充说，这个角色正在接受这位亿万富翁的举止、语气和公开言论，以及他自己最近对公司战略的思考的培训，以便员工通过与创始人的互动，感觉与创始人的联系更加紧密。”

当然，更可能的结果是员工感觉与“创始人”的联系减少，因为他们知道他们很可能只是在与人工智能克隆人交谈？

This is extraordinary.

The FT piece says "They added that the character was being trained on the billionaire’s mannerisms, tone and publicly available statements, as well as his own recent thinking on company strategies, so that employees might feel more connected to the founder through interactions with it."

Surely the more likely outcome is that employees feel less connected to "the founder" because they know that there's a high chance they are simply talking to an AI clone?

在 HN 中查看
empyrrhicist 2026/04/13 22:45
> 通过与创始人的互动，可能会感觉与创始人的联系更加紧密

还有……这是大多数人想要的吗？

> might feel more connected to the founder through interactions with it

Also... is that a thing most people want?

在 HN 中查看
lubujackson 2026/04/13 22:52
Robo-Zuck 似乎是这么认为的……他的人工智能也同意这一点。
Robo-Zuck seems to think so... and his AI agrees.
在 HN 中查看
SpicyLemonZest 2026/04/13 22:52
是的。人们并不总是把它描述为“哦，如果我能见到马克·扎克伯格就好了”，但大多数人至少对与首席执行官关系友好的公司有一点渴望。

这是一个有意义的替代吗？可能不会，但我不准备排除这种可能性。如果给千分之一的克劳德一个扎克伯格的形象，我敢打赌你会从中得到一些欢笑。

Yes. People don’t always frame it as “ooh, if only I could meet Mark Zuckerberg”, but most people IME are at least a little wistful about the kind of company where you’re on friendly terms with your CEO.

Is this a meaningful replacement for that? Probably not, but I’m not prepared to rule it out. Give 1 in 1000 Claudes a Zuckerberg persona and you’d get some chuckles out of it I bet.

在 HN 中查看
MagicMoonlight 2026/04/13 23:00
我的意思是，最大的问题是当他们说服他的模型使用 N 字或发布一些公开声明时。这只是灾难的根源。
I mean, the biggest issue is when they persuade his model to use the N-word or make some public announcement. It's just a recipe for disaster.

e12e 2026/04/13 22:54

https://archive.is/mtVXJ

throwworhtthrow 2026/04/13 23:47
如果您是那种会查看此类标题帖子评论的人，那么您可能还想（重新）观看马克后院熏肉派对的 2 分钟精彩片段。 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eBxTEoseZak
If you're the type of person who checks the comments on a post with this kind of headline, then you probably also want to (re-)watch the 2 minute highlight reel of Mark's backyard meat-smoking party. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eBxTEoseZak
在 HN 中查看
billybuckwheat 2026/04/14 03:05
早在 20 世纪 80 年代，一些日本公司就有房间，你可以在里面用竹刀敲击老板的雕像。只是为了释放一点蒸汽。 Meta 的工作人员能够用扎克伯格的人工智能克隆来做类似的事情吗？
Back in the 1980s, some Japanese companies had rooms in which you could whack at an effigy of the boss with a shinai. Just to let off a bit of steam. Will Meta's workers be able to do something like this with Zuckerberg's AI clone?
在 HN 中查看
Rekindle8090 2026/04/13 23:21
我在三大超大规模公司之一工作，在我的公司尝试使用人工智能取代的所有事物中，会议、日历管理、电子邮件不是其中之一。

高层对话太多了，可以概括为： “如果我给你发一封电子邮件，而人工智能回复了，我就不想和你一起工作。”

工作中的沟通需要对与他人建立职业关系的含义有一个合理的界限。你选择与某人合作是因为你相信他们的判断、他们的话语、他们在对话中致力于某件事并坚持到底的能力。人工智能克隆人无法承诺任何事情。不能按其所言。它不能拥有决定权。

当您向某人发送电子邮件时，您是在要求与该人交谈。当你与某人开会时，你期待着那个人的关注和判断。尤其是在企业与企业之间，关系就是产品。和AI没有任何关系。

抛开尊重和社会契约不谈，目前人工智能不具备通用智能来执行寻找生产力、连接点、软技能、利益相关者协调等所需的执行功能。

I work at one of the big 3 hyperscalers, and of all the things my company is trying to use AI to replace, meetings, calendar management, emails are NOT one of them.

There have been too many high level conversations that can be summarized as: "If I send you an email, and an AI responds, I do not want to work with you."

Communication at work requires a reasonable boundary about what it means to have a professional relationship with another human being. You chose to work with a person because you trust their judgment, their word, their ability to commit to something in conversation and follow through. An AI clone can't commit to anything. It can't be held to what it said. It can't own a decision.

When you email someone, you are asking to talk to that person. When you sit in a meeting with someone, you are expecting that person's attention and judgment. Especially with business to business, the relationship is the product. There is no relationship with AI.

Respect and social contracts aside, right now, AI does not have the general intelligence to perform the executive function needed to find productivity and connect the dots, soft skill, stakeholder alignment etc.

在 HN 中查看
neko_ranger 2026/04/13 22:43
如果你发明了马克·扎克伯格，你就发明了马克·扎克伯格
if you invented mark zuckerberg, you'd have invented mark zuckerberg

hanyki111 2026/04/13 22:42
人工智能要取代人类，就需要人类思维方式的数字副本。我认为目前的人工智能不可能实现这一点。
For artificial intelligence to replace oneself, it would need a digital copy of one's way of thinking. I believe this is impossible to implement with current AI.

z500 2026/04/13 22:44
不需要人工智能来要求图表上升
It doesn't take an AI to demand graph go up

lostmsu 2026/04/13 23:11

Impossible is a strong word given our collective way of thinking has been reproduced with decent level of approximation.

throwanem 2026/04/13 22:42
你会怎么说？

在 HN 中查看
ZiiS 2026/04/13 22:44

So either the AI clone will make different decisions; or it will also replace itself with an AI clone...

treetalker 2026/04/14 00:02

Unsatisfied with automating programming, Meta has successfully automated comedy.
在 HN 中查看
nickvec 2026/04/14 01:51
你能做出这样的事，是多么的不合时宜呢？
How out of touch can you be to do something like this?
在 HN 中查看
FrankWilhoit 2026/04/13 23:22
这样，每一个决定都是可否认的。
This way, every decision is deniable.
在 HN 中查看
financetechbro 2026/04/14 01:03
当扎克停产时会发生什么？他是否会将他的 Meta 股份转移到人工智能克隆人拥有的信托基金中？这是否意味着我们将不得不永远与扎克打交道？
What happens when Zuck is EOL? Does he transfer his Meta shares to a trust owned by the AI clone? Does that mean that we will have to deal with Zuck for literally forever??
在 HN 中查看
dwa3592 2026/04/13 22:45
对于人工智能来说可能太机械化了！
might be too robotic for the AI!
在 HN 中查看
claudiulodro 2026/04/14 02:33
首席执行官的角色基本上是做出艰难的决定，而不是在会议上表现得友好。

如果人工智能克隆人没有权力代表扎克伯格做出决定，那还有什么意义呢？如果它有权代表他做出决定，那么谁对其做出的错误决定负责？

The role of a CEO is basically to make tough decisions, not really to be some sort of friendly face in meetings.

If the AI clone is not empowered to make decisions on Zuck's behalf, what's the point? If it is empowered to make decisions on his behalf, who is accountable for bad decisions it makes?

martythemaniak 2026/04/13 22:37

Well, directly managing all your employees is the new hot trend and how else are you gonna do it?

thewileyone 2026/04/14 10:28

lostmsu 2026/04/13 23:09

Meetings? That's not interesting. I'm working on replacing myself (or rather my body) after death in a ship of Theseus manner.

shevy-java 2026/04/13 22:59

etchalon 2026/04/13 22:49

在 HN 中查看
oulipo2 2026/04/13 22:47
等待！！他已经不是机器人了？
Wait!! He's not ALREADY a robot?
在 HN 中查看
alex1138 2026/04/13 22:43
我从来没见过他这么诚实。无论你如何看待个别事件（剑桥分析是真正的政治丑闻还是只是数据丑闻？或者其他什么），他只是……不是……好吧，他是一个“粗心的人”（https://www.amazon.com/Careless-People-Cautionary-Power-Idea...）Facebook 的整个历史就是他们要么改变帖子设置的隐私，要么改变 TOS（地毯拉动），要么人们无法看到彼此的帖子（https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=14147719, https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=32826437）或无法联系对方（https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=4151433, https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=6090712）
I've never seen him as honest. Whatever you think of individual incidents (was Cambridge Analytica a real scandal politically or just a data scandal? - or whatever) he's just... not a... well, he's a "careless person" (https://www.amazon.com/Careless-People-Cautionary-Power-Idea...) Facebook's entire history is them either changing privacy on post settings, or changing TOS (rug pulling) or people not being able to either see each others posts (https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=14147719, https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=32826437) or not being able to contact each other (https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=4151433, https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=6090712)
在 HN 中查看
stogot 2026/04/13 22:38
听起来像是 3、2、1 即将发生的股东诉讼。
Sounds like a shareholder lawsuit coming in 3, 2, 1.
在 HN 中查看
fmbb 2026/04/13 22:48
但如何或为何？

扎克伯格在上市公司的首席执行官中拥有独特的权力。他控制着董事会并拥有多数投票权股份。

当然，理论上他们可以起诉他对公司的某种严重管理不善或不忠诚，但所有者阶层为什么要这么做呢？投资者都在押注人工智能取代人类工人。如果他们认为扎克伯格这样做是错误的，他们会暗示人工智能不应该代替人类工作。

How or why though?

Zuckerberg has unique power among CEOs in public companies. He controls the board and he owns a majority of voting shares.

Sure they can theoretically sue him for some kind of gross mismanagement of the company or disloyalty, but why would the owner class do that? Investors are all in on AI replacing human workers. If they think Zuckerberg doing this is wrong, they would imply AI should not work in place of humans.

在 HN 中查看
JumpCrisscross 2026/04/14 03:40
> 理论上他们可以起诉他对公司的某种严重管理不善或不忠诚

他们实际上只能以违反信托义务为由提起诉讼。扎克伯格控制着多数，但滥用少数仍是有限度的。我不确定克隆人工智能是否违反任何规则。

> they can theoretically sue him for some kind of gross mismanagement of the company or disloyalty

They can really only sue for breach of fiduciary duty. Zuckerberg controls the majority, but there are still limits on abusing the minority. I’m not sure making an AI clone falls afoul of any rules.
